title: crepis foetida var. glandulosa
intro: Crepis foetida var. glandulosa, also known as the hairy hawk's beard or stinking hawk's beard, is a plant species belonging to the dandelion family and is native to Europe and Asia. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties, making it a potential treatment for various health issues. The health benefits of Crepis foetida var. glandulosa include its potential to aid in digestion, reduce inflammation, and boost the immune system. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and its leaves and roots can be used to make a medicinal extract.
